crash.rs:

  panic/crash messages, replacing inline logic
- Fix Unicode correctness: use s.chars().count() instead of s.len()
  for the 200-character limit, preventing mid-codepoint truncation

- Normalize import ordering: runtime_compat imports grouped consistently
- Align multi-line method chains (RuntimeBuilder.build().unwrap()) for
  readability across all call sites

 {"id":"ft-dr6zv.1","title":"Integrate FrankenSearch into FrankenTerm — replace DIY search with production library","description":"# Integrate FrankenSearch into FrankenTerm

## Vision
Replace FrankenTerm's current hand-rolled search module (`search/mod.rs` with its own embedder, chunking, hybrid_search, reranker) with the production-grade `frankensearch` library. FrankenSearch provides two-tier progressive delivery (fast results in ~15ms, quality-refined in ~150ms), FSVI vector format with f16 quantization and SIMD, Tantivy BM25, RRF fusion, and an embedder stack with automatic fallback — all battle-tested across three production codebases.

## Current State
FrankenTerm already has a `search/` module with its own hybrid search, chunking, embedder, and reranker code. This is a parallel implementation of what FrankenSearch does better and more robustly. The integration replaces the DIY version with the library.

## Key Integration Challenge
**Runtime mismatch**: FrankenSearch uses `asupersync` (structured concurrency, no Tokio), while FrankenTerm uses Tokio. A runtime bridge adapter is required. FrankenTerm already has an epic (`ft-brc7d`) for asupersync migration, so this integration should use feature-gated adapters that work with either runtime.

## Integration Surface
- Replace `crates/frankenterm-core/src/search/` internals with frankensearch facade
- Wire `TwoTierSearcher` progressive callbacks into TUI search overlay
- Index terminal scrollback, session recordings, and agent output
- Expose via robot mode CLI and IPC status API
- Feature-gate behind `frankensearch` feature flag

## Success Criteria
- All existing search functionality preserved or improved
- Progressive delivery visible in TUI (fast results appear, then refine)
- Search latency p50 < 20ms for fast tier across 10K+ indexed documents
- Zero regression in pane rendering performance (search runs off hot path)
- Graceful degradation when search index unavailable","status":"open","priority":1,"issue_type":"epic","estimated_minutes":1200,"created_at":"2026-02-16T04:48:48.254684Z","created_by":"jemanuel","updated_at":"2026-02-16T04:48:48.254684Z","source_repo":".","compaction_level":0,"original_size":0,"labels":["frankensearch","integration","search","track-search"],"dependencies":[{"issue_id":"ft-dr6zv.1","depends_on_id":"ft-dr6zv","type":"parent-child","created_at":"2026-02-16T04:48:48.254684Z","created_by":"jemanuel","metadata":"{}","thread_id":""}]}
 {"id":"ft-dr6zv.1.1","title":"Add frankensearch workspace dependency with feature gate","description":"## Context
FrankenSearch needs to be added as a workspace dependency and feature-gated in frankenterm-core so it can be optionally compiled.

## Scope
1. Add `frankensearch` to workspace `[dependencies]` in root Cargo.toml as a git dependency pointing to the frankensearch repo
2. Select appropriate feature set: start with `hybrid` (hash + model2vec + fastembed + lexical) for full two-tier capability
3. Add `frankensearch` feature flag to frankenterm-core/Cargo.toml gating the dependency
4. Add `frankensearch` to the default feature set (or a `full` preset) so it builds by default
5. Create `#[cfg(feature = "frankensearch")]` module declaration in lib.rs
6. Verify workspace builds clean with `cargo check --workspace`
7. Verify builds also work WITHOUT the feature: `cargo check -p frankenterm-core --no-default-features`

## Key Considerations
- frankensearch depends on `asupersync` — verify no version conflicts with any existing asupersync references in the workspace (ft-e34d9 migration may have introduced asupersync already)
- frankensearch pulls in `tantivy` — check for conflicts with frankenterm's existing tantivy usage in `tantivy_ingest.rs`, `tantivy_policy.rs`, `tantivy_query.rs`. Goal: single tantivy version in Cargo.lock
- frankensearch uses Rust 2024 edition — verify frankenterm's toolchain is compatible (edition is per-crate, so this should work, but nightly features may be needed)
- Document the feature flag in Cargo.toml with inline comments explaining what it enables

## Testing Requirements
- `test_feature_flag_on`: `cargo check --workspace` passes with frankensearch feature enabled
- `test_feature_flag_off`: `cargo check -p frankenterm-core --no-default-features` passes cleanly
- `test_no_duplicate_tantivy`: `cargo tree -d | grep tantivy` shows zero duplicates
- `test_no_duplicate_asupersync`: `cargo tree -d | grep asupersync` shows zero duplicates
- `test_cfg_module_accessible`: frankensearch types importable when feature enabled
- `test_cfg_module_absent`: frankensearch types NOT importable when feature disabled (no compile error, just absent)
- Run these checks as a CI script: `tests/ci/check_frankensearch_feature.sh`

## Acceptance Criteria
- `cargo check --workspace` passes with frankensearch feature enabled
- `cargo check -p frankenterm-core --no-default-features` passes without it
- No duplicate tantivy versions in Cargo.lock
- No duplicate asupersync versions in Cargo.lock
- Feature flag documented in Cargo.toml with inline comments
- CI script validates both feature-on and feature-off builds","notes":"Implemented workspace-level FrankenSearch integration with feature gating and lockfile convergence:
- Root `Cargo.toml`: added `frankensearch` workspace dependency (git rev `3ad4326f...`, feature `hybrid`, `default-features = false`).
- Root `Cargo.toml`: added `[patch."https://github.com/Dicklesworthstone/frankensearch"]` pinning `asupersync` to `c7c15f63...` to avoid transitive drift.
- Root `Cargo.toml`: aligned workspace `tantivy` to `0.22` so frankenterm + frankensearch resolve to one tantivy major.
- `crates/frankenterm-core/Cargo.toml`: added optional dep `frankensearch`, feature `frankensearch = ["dep:frankensearch"]`, and made it default-on (`default = ["frankensearch"]`) with inline comments.
- `crates/frankenterm-core/src/lib.rs`: added `#[cfg(feature = "frankensearch")]` module wiring (`search_bridge`) as integration placeholder for follow-on runtime bridge bead (`ft-dr6zv.1.2`).

Validation (offloaded via `/Users/jemanuel/bin/rch10 exec -- ...`):
1) `cargo check -p frankenterm-core --lib` => PASS (exit=0)
2) `cargo check -p frankenterm-core --no-default-features` => PASS (exit=0)
3) `cargo check --workspace` => PASS (exit=0)

Duplicate-version sanity:
- `rg "^name = "tantivy"$" Cargo.lock` => single entry
- `rg "^name = "asupersync"$" Cargo.lock` => single entry
- `cargo tree -d --workspace | rg "^tantivy v|^asupersync v"` => no matches

 {"id":"ft-dr6zv.1.2","title":"asupersync↔Tokio runtime bridge adapter","description":"## Context
FrankenSearch uses `asupersync` for all async operations (every method takes `&Cx`), while FrankenTerm uses Tokio. A runtime bridge adapter is needed to call frankensearch APIs from Tokio task contexts.

**Related prior work:**
- `ft-e34d9` (IN_PROGRESS): Epic to replace Tokio with asupersync across all of FrankenTerm
- `ft-1j0ye` (CLOSED): Dual-runtime scaffold — `runtime_compat.rs` already exists with feature-flagged Mutex/RwLock/channel/spawn abstractions

## Scope
Create a `search_bridge.rs` module in frankenterm-core that:
1. Wraps `TwoTierSearcher` methods with Tokio-compatible async interfaces
2. Manages an asupersync `Cx` (capability context) lifecycle within a Tokio task
3. Handles cancellation propagation between Tokio CancellationToken and asupersync structured cancellation
4. Provides a `SearchBridge` struct that owns the searcher and exposes `async fn search()` returning Tokio futures
5. Uses existing `runtime_compat.rs` (from ft-1j0ye) abstractions where applicable

## Key Design Decision
Use `tokio::task::spawn_blocking` to run asupersync's event loop on a blocking thread, with channel-based result passing back to the Tokio runtime. This avoids nested runtime issues and keeps the integration clean.

## Alternative: Feature-gated dual path
When ft-e34d9 (asupersync migration) completes, the bridge becomes unnecessary — frankensearch can be called directly via native asupersync Cx. Design the bridge as a thin shim behind `#[cfg(not(feature = "asupersync-runtime"))]` that gets compiled out when the migration is done. The `#[cfg(feature = "asupersync-runtime")]` path calls FrankenSearch directly without the bridge.

## Testing Requirements
- `test_bridge_round_trip`: spawn_blocking correctly passes query and returns results
- `test_bridge_cancellation_forward`: Tokio CancellationToken → asupersync Cx cancellation
- `test_bridge_cancellation_reverse`: asupersync cancel → Tokio task cancelled
- `test_bridge_timeout`: search exceeding deadline returns error, never hangs
- `test_bridge_concurrent_searches`: 10 parallel searches, no deadlocks
- `test_bridge_overhead`: bridge adds < 1ms beyond raw FrankenSearch latency
- All tests use structured JSON logging with `test_name`, `phase`, `duration_ms`, `result`

## Acceptance Criteria
- FrankenSearch `TwoTierSearcher::search()` callable from Tokio async context
- Progressive callbacks (`SearchPhase::Initial`, `Refined`, `RefinementFailed`) propagated to caller
- Cancellation works in both directions
- No deadlocks under concurrent search + pane rendering
- Bridge adds < 1ms overhead beyond the search itself
- Bridge compiles out cleanly when asupersync-runtime feature is enabled (direct path used instead)
- All unit tests pass with structured logging output","status":"closed","priority":1,"issue_type":"task","assignee":"CrimsonReef","estimated_minutes":240,"created_at":"2026-02-16T04:53:34.808007Z","created_by":"jemanuel","updated_at":"2026-02-16T05:56:13.489443Z","closed_at":"2026-02-16T05:56:13.489429Z","close_reason":"Implemented SearchBridge module + lib wiring; targeted lib checks passed (default and asupersync-runtime). -{"id":"ft-dr6zv.1.3","title":"Replace existing search module with FrankenSearch backend","description":"## Context
FrankenTerm's existing `search/` module is a substantial implementation (~200KB across 12 files) with its own hybrid search, chunking, embedder stack, reranker, vector index, and background daemon. FrankenSearch provides all of this more robustly. The migration must be careful, incremental, and regression-tested — this is NOT a trivial swap.

## Existing search/ module inventory (what's being replaced)
- `mod.rs` (1.6KB) — orchestration
- `hybrid_search.rs` (25KB) — RRF fusion, multi-signal search
- `embedder.rs` (11KB) — embedder trait + dispatch
- `hash_embedder.rs` (14KB) — locality-sensitive hashing
- `fastembed_embedder.rs` (1.1KB) — fastembed wrapper
- `model2vec_embedder.rs` (1.1KB) — model2vec wrapper
- `model_registry.rs` (1.3KB) — model catalog
- `chunk_vector_store.rs` (52KB) — HNSW vector index with persistence
- `chunking.rs` (60KB) — text chunking with overlap strategies
- `reranker.rs` (7.8KB) — FlashRank cross-encoder reranking
- `vector_index.rs` (19KB) — FSVI-like vector storage
- `daemon/` — background indexing service

## Scope
Replace internals of these files while preserving their public API contracts:
- `search/mod.rs` — replace orchestration with `TwoTierSearcher` delegation via SearchBridge
- `search/hybrid_search.rs` — replace with frankensearch-fusion RRF
- `search/embedder.rs` — replace with frankensearch-embed `EmbedderStack`
- `search/chunk_vector_store.rs` — replace with frankensearch-index FSVI
- `search/chunking.rs` — evaluate overlap with frankensearch-storage; keep FrankenTerm-specific chunking strategies (terminal output has different structure than prose)
- `search/reranker.rs` — replace with frankensearch-rerank (FlashRank cross-encoder)
- `search/vector_index.rs` — replace with frankensearch FSVI format
- `search/daemon/` — adapt to use frankensearch background indexing APIs

The existing `SearchConfig` in `config.rs` should be extended to support FrankenSearch-specific settings (rrf_k, quality_weight, quality_timeout_ms, fast_only mode, embedder tier selection).

## Migration Strategy (incremental, not big-bang)
1. **Phase A**: Add FrankenSearch as alternative backend behind cfg flag — old code still compiles and runs
2. **Phase B**: Wire each sub-module to delegate to FrankenSearch when flag enabled, keeping old impl as fallback
3. **Phase C**: Run regression suite comparing old vs new results (see testing section)
4. **Phase D**: Remove old implementations once regression suite passes at 100%
5. **Phase E**: Clean up compat shims, remove dead code

## Key Decisions
- Keep the existing search module's public types as a compatibility layer that delegates to FrankenSearch internals. This prevents breaking changes for existing robot mode consumers and TUI code.
- Terminal-specific chunking strategies (blank-line boundaries, prompt detection, ANSI-aware splitting) may NOT exist in FrankenSearch — preserve FrankenTerm's custom chunkers and feed their output into FrankenSearch's index.
- Existing tantivy_ingest.rs / tantivy_query.rs must be unified with frankensearch-lexical to avoid running two Tantivy instances.

## Testing Requirements
- **Regression suite**: Capture current search behavior (queries + expected results) BEFORE migration. After each migration phase, verify identical or better results.
- `test_api_compat`: all public types from pre-FrankenSearch API still compile (compile-time test)
- `test_rrf_fusion_correctness`: RRF scores match for known inputs
- `test_embedder_stack_fallback`: hash → model2vec → fastembed tier cascade works
- `test_config_knobs`: rrf_k, quality_weight, quality_timeout_ms, fast_only all take effect
- `test_tantivy_no_duplication`: single Tantivy index, not two parallel ones
- `test_search_daemon_uses_frankensearch`: daemon/ background indexing calls frankensearch APIs
- `test_existing_robot_search_output_format`: `ft robot search` output format unchanged
- All tests use structured JSON logging

## Acceptance Criteria
- All existing `ft robot search` commands produce equivalent or better results
- SearchConfig extended with FrankenSearch tuning knobs
- Existing tantivy_ingest/tantivy_query code unified with frankensearch-lexical (single Tantivy instance)
- No public API breaking changes in robot mode output format
- Search daemon mode adapted to use frankensearch background indexing
- Old search implementations removable once regression suite passes (clean phase D)
- Zero regressions verified by automated regression suite with detailed diff logging","notes":"Phase A slice complete by OrangeEagle: added opt-in frankensearch fusion backend path in search/hybrid_search.rs with env selector (FT_SEARCH_FUSION_BACKEND), preserved legacy weighted fallback, re-exported FusionBackend in search/mod.rs, and added backend-specific tests. +{"id":"ft-dr6zv.1.3","title":"Replace existing search module with FrankenSearch backend","description":"## Context
FrankenTerm's existing `search/` module is a substantial implementation (~200KB across 12 files) with its own hybrid search, chunking, embedder stack, reranker, vector index, and background daemon. FrankenSearch provides all of this more robustly. The migration must be careful, incremental, and regression-tested — this is NOT a trivial swap.

## Existing search/ module inventory (what's being replaced)
- `mod.rs` (1.6KB) — orchestration
- `hybrid_search.rs` (25KB) — RRF fusion, multi-signal search
- `embedder.rs` (11KB) — embedder trait + dispatch
- `hash_embedder.rs` (14KB) — locality-sensitive hashing
- `fastembed_embedder.rs` (1.1KB) — fastembed wrapper
- `model2vec_embedder.rs` (1.1KB) — model2vec wrapper
- `model_registry.rs` (1.3KB) — model catalog
- `chunk_vector_store.rs` (52KB) — HNSW vector index with persistence
- `chunking.rs` (60KB) — text chunking with overlap strategies
- `reranker.rs` (7.8KB) — FlashRank cross-encoder reranking
- `vector_index.rs` (19KB) — FSVI-like vector storage
- `daemon/` — background indexing service

## Scope
Replace internals of these files while preserving their public API contracts:
- `search/mod.rs` — replace orchestration with `TwoTierSearcher` delegation via SearchBridge
- `search/hybrid_search.rs` — replace with frankensearch-fusion RRF
- `search/embedder.rs` — replace with frankensearch-embed `EmbedderStack`
- `search/chunk_vector_store.rs` — replace with frankensearch-index FSVI
- `search/chunking.rs` — evaluate overlap with frankensearch-storage; keep FrankenTerm-specific chunking strategies (terminal output has different structure than prose)
- `search/reranker.rs` — replace with frankensearch-rerank (FlashRank cross-encoder)
- `search/vector_index.rs` — replace with frankensearch FSVI format
- `search/daemon/` — adapt to use frankensearch background indexing APIs

The existing `SearchConfig` in `config.rs` should be extended to support FrankenSearch-specific settings (rrf_k, quality_weight, quality_timeout_ms, fast_only mode, embedder tier selection).

## Migration Strategy (incremental, not big-bang)
1. **Phase A**: Add FrankenSearch as alternative backend behind cfg flag — old code still compiles and runs
2. **Phase B**: Wire each sub-module to delegate to FrankenSearch when flag enabled, keeping old impl as fallback
3. **Phase C**: Run regression suite comparing old vs new results (see testing section)
4. **Phase D**: Remove old implementations once regression suite passes at 100%
5. **Phase E**: Clean up compat shims, remove dead code

## Key Decisions
- Keep the existing search module's public types as a compatibility layer that delegates to FrankenSearch internals. This prevents breaking changes for existing robot mode consumers and TUI code.
- Terminal-specific chunking strategies (blank-line boundaries, prompt detection, ANSI-aware splitting) may NOT exist in FrankenSearch — preserve FrankenTerm's custom chunkers and feed their output into FrankenSearch's index.
- Existing tantivy_ingest.rs / tantivy_query.rs must be unified with frankensearch-lexical to avoid running two Tantivy instances.

## Testing Requirements
- **Regression suite**: Capture current search behavior (queries + expected results) BEFORE migration. After each migration phase, verify identical or better results.
- `test_api_compat`: all public types from pre-FrankenSearch API still compile (compile-time test)
- `test_rrf_fusion_correctness`: RRF scores match for known inputs
- `test_embedder_stack_fallback`: hash → model2vec → fastembed tier cascade works
- `test_config_knobs`: rrf_k, quality_weight, quality_timeout_ms, fast_only all take effect
- `test_tantivy_no_duplication`: single Tantivy index, not two parallel ones
- `test_search_daemon_uses_frankensearch`: daemon/ background indexing calls frankensearch APIs
- `test_existing_robot_search_output_format`: `ft robot search` output format unchanged
- All tests use structured JSON logging

## Acceptance Criteria
- All existing `ft robot search` commands produce equivalent or better results
- SearchConfig extended with FrankenSearch tuning knobs
- Existing tantivy_ingest/tantivy_query code unified with frankensearch-lexical (single Tantivy instance)
- No public API breaking changes in robot mode output format
- Search daemon mode adapted to use frankensearch background indexing
- Old search implementations removable once regression suite passes (clean phase D)
- Zero regressions verified by automated regression suite with detailed diff logging","notes":"Phase A slice complete by OrangeEagle: added opt-in frankensearch fusion backend path in search/hybrid_search.rs with env selector (FT_SEARCH_FUSION_BACKEND), preserved legacy weighted fallback, re-exported FusionBackend in search/mod.rs, and added backend-specific tests.  {"id":"ft-dr6zv.1.4","title":"Progressive search delivery in TUI","description":"## Context
FrankenSearch's killer feature is progressive delivery: fast results appear in ~15ms via hash/model2vec embeddings, then quality-refined results replace them in ~150ms via fastembed + reranking. FrankenTerm's TUI should surface this progression visually.

## Scope
1. Wire `SearchPhase` callbacks into TUI search overlay:
   - `SearchPhase::Initial` → immediately render results with a "refining..." indicator
   - `SearchPhase::Refined` → replace results, remove indicator, show quality improvement delta (e.g., "3 results reranked")
   - `SearchPhase::RefinementFailed` → keep initial results, show subtle warning ("refinement unavailable")
2. Display search tier metadata: which embedder produced each result, latency breakdown per tier
3. Support `fast_only` mode toggle in TUI for latency-sensitive contexts (keybind to toggle)
4. Progressive results in robot mode: emit JSONL with phase markers for agent consumption
5. Result provenance indicators: each result shows [L] for lexical match, [S] for semantic, [F] for fused

## UX Details
- First results should appear while the user is still typing (debounced, ~100ms after last keystroke)
- Quality refinement runs in background, doesn't block UI
- Results should animate/transition smoothly when refined results arrive (no full-screen repaint)
- Show per-result provenance (lexical match, semantic match, or fused) via subtle prefix icons
- Keyboard navigation: arrow keys scroll results, Enter opens result in pane, Tab switches between result and preview
- Result count + timing shown in status bar: "5 results (12ms fast, 143ms refined)"

## Testing Requirements
- `test_initial_results_within_50ms`: fast-tier results rendered before quality-tier completes (timed)
- `test_refinement_updates_display`: mock refined callback → verify display updates with new results
- `test_refinement_failure_graceful`: mock RefinementFailed → verify initial results kept + warning shown
- `test_fast_only_toggle`: toggle fast_only → verify only Initial phase results shown
- `test_phase_markers_jsonl`: robot mode output includes `"phase":"initial"` and `"phase":"refined"` fields
- `test_result_provenance_labels`: each result shows correct [L]/[S]/[F] label
- `test_debounced_search`: rapid keystrokes → only last query executes (not flooding)
- `test_keyboard_navigation`: arrow/enter/tab keybinds navigate results correctly
- `test_no_ui_jank`: search runs on dedicated task, main render loop never blocks > 16ms
- `test_status_bar_timing`: status bar shows correct result count and tier latencies
- All tests use structured JSON logging with timing metrics per phase

## Acceptance Criteria
- TUI search shows initial results within 50ms of query submission
- Refined results update the display within 200ms without flicker or full repaint
- Robot mode JSONL output includes phase field for progressive consumers
- fast_only toggle works in TUI and robot mode
- No UI jank during refinement (search runs on dedicated thread/task, render loop stays < 16ms)
- Result provenance visible to user ([L]/[S]/[F] indicators)
- Keyboard navigation works (arrow keys, Enter, Tab)
- All tests pass with structured logging","status":"in_progress","priority":2,"issue_type":"task","assignee":"FrostyBeaver","estimated_minutes":240,"created_at":"2026-02-16T04:53:44.689656Z","created_by":"jemanuel","updated_at":"2026-02-20T01:39:06.123132Z","source_repo":".","compaction_level":0,"original_size":0,"labels":["frankensearch","tui","ux"],"dependencies":[{"issue_id":"ft-dr6zv.1.4","depends_on_id":"ft-dr6zv.1","type":"parent-child","created_at":"2026-02-16T04:53:44.689656Z","created_by":"jemanuel","metadata":"{}","thread_id":""},{"issue_id":"ft-dr6zv.1.4","depends_on_id":"ft-dr6zv.1.3","type":"blocks","created_at":"2026-02-16T04:54:22.271962Z","created_by":"jemanuel","metadata":"{}","thread_id":""}],"comments":[{"id":518,"issue_id":"ft-dr6zv.1.4","author":"Jeff Emanuel","text":"FrostyBeaver progress slice: implemented TUI progressive-search lifecycle/status plumbing without touching search backend/main.rs.

 {"id":"ft-dr6zv.1.5","title":"Terminal content indexing pipeline for FrankenSearch","description":"## Context
FrankenSearch needs documents to index. In FrankenTerm's context, the indexable corpus is: terminal scrollback buffers, session recordings, agent output logs, pane histories, and command histories. The indexing pipeline must run incrementally without impacting terminal rendering performance.

**Interaction with cass (ft-2l9kn):** The cass integration also indexes agent session data via its own Tantivy FTS index. To avoid duplicate results and wasted resources, the indexing pipeline must coordinate with cass:
- Content indexed by cass (full session transcripts) should NOT be re-indexed by FrankenSearch
- FrankenSearch indexes terminal-specific content that cass doesn't handle (scrollback, command output blocks, pane metadata)
- When both are present, search results are deduped by content hash before display

## Scope
1. Define indexable document types and their extraction from FrankenTerm internals:
   - Scrollback lines (grouped into logical chunks by blank-line boundaries or time gaps)
   - Command output blocks (delimited by prompt patterns, OSC 133 markers)
   - Pane metadata (title, working directory, agent type, process tree) — indexed as facets, not full-text
   - Agent-specific artifacts (tool call results, error messages, code blocks) — extracted via pattern engine
   - NOT indexed: raw ANSI escape sequences, cursor movement sequences, screen state snapshots

2. Implement incremental indexing via `IndexBuilder`:
   - Content-hash dedup (SHA-256 of normalized text — don't re-embed unchanged content)
   - Background indexing on idle (when no active resize/reflow in any pane)
   - Configurable index location (default: `~/.ft/search_index/`)
   - Document source tagging: each indexed document tagged with source (scrollback, command, agent, cass) for filtering

3. Index lifecycle management:
   - Max index size limits (configurable, default 500MB)
   - TTL-based expiration for old sessions (default: 30 days)
   - LRU eviction when size limit reached
   - Manual reindex command via robot mode
   - Index compaction (Tantivy segment merging) on schedule

4. Cass coordination (when ft-2l9kn is also active):
   - Query cass index for content hashes before indexing → skip duplicates
   - Unified search results merge cass + FrankenSearch results via RRF
   - If cass unavailable, FrankenSearch operates standalone (graceful degradation)

## Performance Contract
- Indexing must never block pane rendering (run on dedicated low-priority thread with yield points)
- Incremental updates should process < 100 documents/second without noticeable CPU impact
- Index open (mmap) should add < 5ms to startup
- Background indexing pauses during resize storms (yield to hot path via frame budget system)
- Index writes batched: flush every 5 seconds or 50 documents, whichever comes first

## Testing Requirements
- `test_scrollback_indexing`: known terminal output → indexed → searchable within 1 second
- `test_content_hash_dedup`: same content submitted twice → indexed only once
- `test_incremental_update`: new content added → only delta indexed, not full re-index
- `test_ttl_expiration`: content older than TTL removed from index on next cleanup pass
- `test_index_size_limit`: when limit reached, oldest content evicted first
- `test_indexing_yields_during_resize`: inject resize event → verify indexing pauses
- `test_index_persistence`: write index, restart, verify all content still searchable
- `test_cass_dedup_integration`: mock cass index with content → verify FrankenSearch skips those docs
- `test_document_source_tagging`: verify each document has correct source facet
- `test_batch_flush`: verify 50 documents triggers flush, 5 second timer triggers flush
- All tests use structured JSON logging with timing metrics per phase

## Acceptance Criteria
- Terminal scrollback searchable via FrankenSearch within 1 second of output
- Index persists across FrankenTerm restarts
- Index size stays within configured limits via automatic expiration
- `ft robot search-index stats` shows index health metrics (size, doc count, freshness, segment count)
- Background indexing pauses during resize storms (yield to hot path)
- No duplicate results when both cass and FrankenSearch are active
- Content-hash dedup prevents redundant embedding computation
- All unit and integration tests pass with structured logging","status":"in_progress","priority":1,"issue_type":"task","assignee":"MagentaBeaver","estimated_minutes":300,"created_at":"2026-02-16T04:53:46.298641Z","created_by":"jemanuel","updated_at":"2026-02-19T22:08:18.591542Z","source_repo":".","compaction_level":0,"original_size":0,"labels":["frankensearch","indexing","pipeline"],"dependencies":[{"issue_id":"ft-dr6zv.1.5","depends_on_id":"ft-dr6zv.1","type":"parent-child","created_at":"2026-02-16T04:53:46.298641Z","created_by":"jemanuel","metadata":"{}","thread_id":""},{"issue_id":"ft-dr6zv.1.5","depends_on_id":"ft-dr6zv.1.3","type":"blocks","created_at":"2026-02-16T04:54:23.114215Z","created_by":"jemanuel","metadata":"{}","thread_id":""}],"comments":[{"id":384,"issue_id":"ft-dr6zv.1.5","author":"Jeff Emanuel","text":"Cross-reference: ft-2l9kn (cass integration, IN_PROGRESS) indexes similar content.  {"id":"ft-dr6zv.1.6","title":"IPC and robot mode search API","description":"## Context
FrankenSearch results and index state need to be exposed via FrankenTerm's IPC socket and robot mode CLI for agent consumption and external tooling.

## Scope
1. IPC status API additions:
   - `search_index` field in status payload: index size (bytes), document count, last update timestamp, embedder tiers available, segment count
   - `search_health` field: index freshness (seconds since last update), any indexing errors (count + last error message), background job status (idle/indexing/rebuilding/paused)
2. Robot mode commands:
   - `ft robot search "query" --limit N --format json` — progressive search (returns JSONL with phase markers)
   - `ft robot search "query" --format jsonl` — streaming JSONL, one line per result, phase markers between tiers
   - `ft robot search-index stats` — index health and metrics (JSON)
   - `ft robot search-index rebuild` — trigger full reindex (returns immediately, status via stats)
   - `ft robot search-index pause/resume` — control background indexing
3. Output format support: JSON (complete result set), JSONL (streaming per-result), TOON (via tru integration from ft-165vw)
4. Search explain mode: `ft robot search "query" --explain` returns per-result scoring breakdown:
   - RRF rank and fused score
   - Lexical BM25 score and matching terms
   - Semantic similarity score and embedder tier used
   - Reranker cross-encoder score (if quality tier completed)
5. Backward compatibility: existing `ft robot search` output format preserved — new fields added, no fields removed

## Testing Requirements
- `test_ipc_status_search_index`: status payload includes search_index with all required fields
- `test_ipc_status_search_health`: health field includes freshness, error count, job status
- `test_robot_search_json`: `--format json` returns valid JSON array with required fields per result
- `test_robot_search_jsonl`: `--format jsonl` returns valid JSONL with phase markers between tiers
- `test_robot_search_toon`: `--format toon` returns valid TOON (requires ft-165vw tru integration)
- `test_robot_search_explain`: `--explain` includes per-result scoring breakdown with all 4 score types
- `test_robot_search_limit`: `--limit N` caps results at N
- `test_robot_search_index_stats`: stats command returns JSON with size, doc_count, freshness, segment_count
- `test_robot_search_index_rebuild`: rebuild command triggers reindex, stats shows "rebuilding" status
- `test_robot_search_index_pause_resume`: pause stops indexing, resume restarts it
- `test_graceful_empty_index`: all commands return sensible output when index has zero documents
- `test_graceful_feature_off`: when frankensearch feature disabled, commands return "feature not enabled" message
- `test_backward_compat`: output format matches existing `ft robot search` schema (no removed fields)
- All tests use structured JSON logging with command, args, response_time_ms, status

## Acceptance Criteria
- IPC status includes search index health when frankensearch feature enabled
- Robot mode search returns results in < 50ms (fast tier)
- JSONL streaming output works for progressive delivery
- Explain mode shows full scoring pipeline for debugging relevance
- All commands degrade gracefully when search index is empty or unavailable
- TOON output format supported (via tru integration)
- Backward compatibility with existing robot mode search output maintained
- All tests pass with structured logging","status":"in_progress","priority":2,"issue_type":"task","assignee":"CoralElk","estimated_minutes":240,"created_at":"2026-02-16T04:53:50.803202Z","created_by":"jemanuel","updated_at":"2026-02-19T22:19:43.538171Z","source_repo":".","compaction_level":0,"original_size":0,"labels":["frankensearch","ipc","robot-mode"],"dependencies":[{"issue_id":"ft-dr6zv.1.6","depends_on_id":"ft-dr6zv.1","type":"parent-child","created_at":"2026-02-16T04:53:50.803202Z","created_by":"jemanuel","metadata":"{}","thread_id":""},{"issue_id":"ft-dr6zv.1.6","depends_on_id":"ft-dr6zv.1.3","type":"blocks","created_at":"2026-02-16T04:54:24.517054Z","created_by":"jemanuel","metadata":"{}","thread_id":""}],"comments":[{"id":500,"issue_id":"ft-dr6zv.1.6","author":"Jeff Emanuel","text":"CoralElk progress: implemented bounded robot API slice for search-index stats.  {"id":"ft-e6pq","title":"Layout restoration engine — recreate window/tab/split topology from snapshot","description":"## Goal
Implement the layout restoration engine that recreates the exact window/tab/pane split topology from a MuxSnapshot, positioning panes in the same arrangement they had before the mux server restart.

## Background & Motivation
After a mux server restart, all windows, tabs, and split panes are gone. The restoration engine must recreate the exact same layout using WezTerm CLI commands (wezterm cli spawn, wezterm cli split-pane). This is the visual foundation — users should see the same window arrangement they had before.

WezTerm's resurrect.wezterm plugin does approximate layout restoration via Lua but has limitations: it can't restore exact split ratios, and it struggles with complex nested splits. Our approach uses the vendored mux client and exact pane geometry data captured in the MuxSnapshot.

## Technical Design

### Struct Definition
```rust
// Location: crates/wa-core/src/snapshot/restore_layout.rs
pub struct LayoutRestorer {
    mux_client: Arc<DirectMuxClient>,
    config: RestoreConfig,
}

pub struct RestoreConfig {
    pub restore_working_dirs: bool,  // default: true
    pub restore_split_ratios: bool,  // default: true
    pub restore_window_positions: bool, // default: false (OS may differ)
}

// Maps old pane IDs to new pane IDs after restoration
pub type PaneIdMap = HashMap<PaneId, PaneId>;
```

### Core Algorithm
1. For each window in the snapshot:
   a. Create a new window (wezterm cli spawn --new-window)
   b. Record the new window_id
2. For each tab in the window:
   a. If not the first tab, create new tab (wezterm cli spawn-tab)
   b. Record the new tab_id
3. For the pane tree within each tab:
   a. Use recursive descent on the PaneNode tree
   b. For Split nodes: create split-pane with correct direction (horizontal/vertical)
   c. For Leaf nodes: record the pane_id mapping (old -> new)
   d. Adjust split ratios using resize commands

### Recursive Split Restoration
```rust
async fn restore_pane_tree(
    &self,
    node: &PaneNode,
    parent_pane_id: PaneId,
    id_map: &mut PaneIdMap,
) -> Result<PaneId> {
    match node {
        PaneNode::Leaf { pane_id, cwd, .. } => {
            id_map.insert(*pane_id, parent_pane_id);
            if self.config.restore_working_dirs {
                // Set working directory via shell command
                self.set_cwd(parent_pane_id, cwd).await?;
            }
            Ok(parent_pane_id)
        }
        PaneNode::Split { direction, children, ratio, .. } => {
            // First child uses the existing pane
            let first = self.restore_pane_tree(&children[0], parent_pane_id, id_map).await?;
            // Second child gets a new split pane
            let new_pane = self.create_split(parent_pane_id, *direction).await?;
            let second = self.restore_pane_tree(&children[1], new_pane, id_map).await?;
            // Adjust split ratio
            if self.config.restore_split_ratios {
                self.adjust_ratio(first, second, *ratio).await?;
            }
            Ok(first)
        }
    }
}
```

## Integration Points
- **MuxSnapshot schema** (bd-cuz): Reads PaneNode tree structure
- **DirectMuxClient**: Uses spawn, split-pane, and resize PDUs
- **WeztermClient** (wezterm.rs): Fallback to CLI for operations not in mux protocol
- **PaneIdMap**: Returned to caller for use by scrollback injection and process re-launch

## Key Files to Create/Modify
- CREATE: crates/wa-core/src/snapshot/restore_layout.rs
- MODIFY: crates/wa-core/src/snapshot/mod.rs (add module)
- MODIFY: crates/wa-core/src/vendored/mux_client.rs (may need spawn/split PDU support)

## Edge Cases
- Window manager may not honor exact window positions (especially across restarts)
- Split ratios may need multiple resize iterations to converge
- If mux server has existing panes, restoration should create in addition (not replace)
- Tab titles should be restored from snapshot metadata

## Dependencies
- bd-cuz: MuxSnapshot schema (PaneNode tree definition)

## Acceptance Criteria
- Single window with multiple tabs restores correctly
- Horizontal and vertical splits restore with correct topology
- Nested splits (3+ levels deep) restore correctly
- Split ratios are approximately correct (within 5% of original)
- Working directories are set correctly for each pane
- PaneIdMap correctly maps all old pane IDs to new ones
- Handles graceful errors (e.g., if a split fails, continues with remaining)

## Estimated Effort
2-3 hours implementation, 1 hour testing

## Test Requirements

### Property-Based Testing (proptest)
Add proptest cases in `crates/wa-core/tests/proptest_layout_restore.rs`:
- **Layout roundtrip invariant**: For any arbitrary layout tree, `save(restore(save(layout))) == save(layout)` — saving a restored layout must produce an identical snapshot to the original. This is the fundamental correctness property. Strategy: `arb_layout_tree()` generates random PaneNode trees with 1-50 panes, random split directions, and random ratios in [0.1, 0.9].
- **PaneIdMap completeness**: For any layout tree, after restoration the PaneIdMap must contain exactly one entry for every leaf pane in the original tree (no missing mappings, no extra mappings).
- **Topology preservation**: For any layout tree, the structural shape (split directions, nesting depth, child ordering) of the restored layout must exactly match the original, regardless of specific pane IDs.
- **Split ratio fidelity**: For any layout tree with split ratios, restored ratios must be within 5% of originals. Strategy: `arb_ratio()` generates f64 in [0.1, 0.9].

### Criterion Benchmarks
Add benchmarks in `crates/wa-core/benches/layout_restore.rs`:
- `bench_restore_50_pane_layout`: Restore a 50-pane complex layout (10 tabs, mixed splits), target <500ms
- `bench_restore_single_tab_deep_splits`: Restore a single tab with 10-level deep nested splits
- `bench_pane_id_map_construction`: PaneIdMap construction for 200 panes, target <1ms

## Cross-References
- **wa-2dd4s.3** (Zellij swap layouts): Zellij-style swap layouts provide named layout presets that can be toggled. The layout restoration engine should be aware of swap layout metadata in snapshots so that restored sessions can resume swap-layout cycling from where the user left off.
- **wa-2dd4s.2** (Floating panes): Floating (overlay) panes are not part of the split tree — they exist as separate overlay layers.
